chunk_summary: This section presents case vignettes illustrating follow-up and referral decisions for children with moderate acute malnutrition (MAM), focusing on discordant anthropometric indices, non-response, TB suspicion, and relapse management.
learning_objectives: Understand the management of discordant MUAC and WHZ measurements in MAM cases.
Identify strategies to address non-response due to food sharing and dilution.
Recognize high-risk MAM cases with TB suspicion and initiate appropriate referrals.
Develop follow-up plans for relapse cases in MAM management.
key_facts: MUAC <125 mm or WHZ <-2 are key markers for MAM diagnosis.
WHO high-risk criteria include age under 24 months and low MUAC range.
Non-response may result from household sharing or improper food preparation.
TB suspicion in MAM cases requires referral to the NTEP diagnostic pathway.
Relapse within 2 months of discharge indicates unresolved underlying drivers.
reference_quote: WHO high-risk criteria include age under 24 months and low MUAC range (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK601642/).
